structure.title: Change history of AUTOSAR traceable items 17
AUTOSAR AP R25-11
structure.summary: The section "AUTOSAR AP R25-11" references three subsections detailing changes from R22-11: A.4.7 outlines added requirements, A.4.8 describes changed requirements, and A.4.9 lists deleted requirements. However, the main section "AUTOSAR AP R25-11" itself contains no textual content, only the headings for the preceding subsections. The summary focuses solely on the referenced changes in R22-11, as no details about R25-11 are provided. The document structure indicates R25-11 may be a separate release, but its content is absent in the given text.

nodes.title: Document Conventions
nodes.summary: The representation of requirements in AUTOSAR documents adheres to the table specified in [TPS_STDT_00078], as outlined in the Standardization Template. Verbal forms for expressing obligation, defined in [TPS_STDT_00053], are mandated to indicate requirements, per the Standardization Template. Both conventions ensure consistency in documenting obligations and requirements within AUTOSAR standards. The references to [1, Standardization Template] link these practices to the broader standardization framework. No additional details or external information are included

nodes.title: Requirements
nodes.summary: The "Requirements" section lists three specific requirements: [RS_OSIQ_00204], [RS_OSIQ_00208], and [RS_OSIQ_00209], which are not included in the current release. These requirements are noted as subject to modification and are pending consideration for inclusion in a future document release. The text clarifies that the described functionality is not part of the current implementation but may be addressed in subsequent updates. No additional details about their scope or dependencies are provided.

structure.title: Acronyms and abbreviations
structure.summary: The section provides a glossary of acronyms and abbreviations relevant to the document, organized into two tables. It defines "Operating System" as software managing processes and hardware resources, and "Operating System Interface (OSI)" as the functional cluster in the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform specifying the OS interface for adaptive applications. Other terms like Execution Management, Adaptive Application, ARTI, and Functional Cluster are referenced to external documents (e.g., [2],

structure.title: Requirements Specification
structure.summary: The "Requirements Specification" section outlines the criteria and specifications necessary for selecting an Operating System for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. It details the functional, non-functional, and compatibility requirements specific to the platform's architecture. The chapter emphasizes adherence to AUTOSAR standards and integration with automotive software ecosystems. All technical constraints and selection parameters for the OS are formally defined here. The requirements are structured to ensure system reliability, performance, and scalability in automotive applications.

nodes.title: Functional Overview
nodes.summary: The **Operating System** manages runtime resources (including time) for all applications on the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform, encompassing both **Adaptive Applications** and **Functional Clusters** (implemented as applications). It collaborates with the **Executive Management** for platform initialization and application lifecycle management. The **Operating System Interface** is divided into two categories: one for requirements directly needed by **Adaptive Applications**, and another for mechanisms essential for implementing **Functional Clusters**, such as those required by **Executive Management**. These latter mechanisms are typically difficult or inefficient to implement outside the **Operating System**. The **Executive Management** itself is a notable **Functional Cluster** relying on specific **Operating System** mechanisms.

nodes.title: Assumption of Use
nodes.summary: The "Assumption of Use" section outlines the execution environment of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform, which serves as the context for running Adaptive Applications. These assumptions focus on properties of the applications rather than strict platform requirements, guiding the derivation of further specifications. They help developers verify if their use cases align with the document and identify relevant requirements. The **Operating System** section specifies OS requirements that processes must assume are fulfilled to achieve their intended functionality. These assumptions provide foundational context for ensuring compatibility and proper implementation.

nodes.title: _Supporting Material:_
nodes.summary: The "Supporting Material:" section references IEEE1003.13 [4] and IEEE1003.1 [5], which define the POSIX PSE51 profile and the broader POSIX standard, respectively. These standards underpin the OS compliance requirements, specifying functionalities like synchronized I/O, timers, signals, and threads for embedded systems. The PSE51 profile is highlighted as sufficient for Adaptive Applications due to its limited scope, excluding features like file systems and process forks. The IEEE1003.13 standard directly supports the POSIX compliance mandate,

nodes.title: Operating System Requirements
nodes.summary: The Operating System must provide mechanisms for system memory budgeting, enabling processes to react to external stimuli from devices and ensuring resource availability in multi-process systems. It must also allow configuration of memory and CPU time budgets for individual processes or groups, supporting security, protection against DoS attacks, and resource starvation mitigation. Additionally, the OS must enable binding processes or process groups to specific CPU cores to ensure task schedulability and concurrent execution in multi-core environments. The Execution Management Functional Cluster must be started as the first process to oversee platform initialization and shutdown. These requirements are driven by upstream needs to ensure adaptive applications can respond to external signals and maintain system stability.

structure.title: **[RS_OSI_00204] The Operating System shall support authorized operating system object access for the software entities which are allowed to do so.**
**[RS_OSI_00206] The Operating System shall provide multi-Process support for isolation of applications.**
**[RS_OSI_00207] The Operating System shall provide the capability to share code and data in an implicit manner.**
References
structure.summary: The "References" section lists five key documents: (1) the AUTOSAR standardization template (AUTOSAR_FO_TPS_StandardizationTemplate), (2) requirements for execution management (AUTOSAR_AP_RS_ExecutionManagement), and (3) the AUTOSAR glossary (AUTOSAR_FO_TR_Glossary). It also includes (4) the IEEE standard for POSIX real-time and embedded application support (IEEE 1003.13-2003) and (5) the POSIX Base Specifications, Issue 7 (Open Group, 2023). These references provide foundational standards for automotive software, POSIX compliance, and terminology. URLs are provided for the IEEE and Open Group standards to access their respective specifications.

nodes.title: A.1.1 Added Requirements in R25-11
nodes.summary: The content for the section "A.1.1 Added Requirements in R25-11" is not provided, so a summary cannot be generated. Please supply the text or details from the section to proceed.

nodes.title: A.1.2 Changed Requirements in R25-11
nodes.summary: The section outlines revised requirements for the Operating System Interface in R25-11, including POSIX PSE51 compliance, C++ support, and handling of process-external stimuli. It mandates mechanisms for system memory and CPU time budgeting, process-CPU core binding, and authorized access to OS objects. Additional requirements cover multi-process isolation, implicit code/data sharing, restricted process functionality access, error handling for unrecoverable errors, and CPU time scheduling

nodes.title: A.3 Traceable item history of this document according to AUTOSAR Release R23-11
A.4.9 Deleted Requirements in R22-11
nodes.summary: The section "A.4.9 Deleted Requirements in R22-11" lists two requirements removed in the R22-11 release: [RS_OSI_00103] requiring the Operating System Interface to support C++, and [RS_OSI_00104] mandating support for reacting to process-external stimuli from devices. The third entry, [RS_OSI_NA], appears as a placeholder with no specified heading or detail. These deletions reflect changes to the system's interface capabilities in the updated requirements. No additional context or rationale for the deletions is provided in the text.
